channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: Towards Privacy-Guaranteed Label Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning: Few-Shot Forgetting without Disclosure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the first method for label unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning (VFL), treating labels as both inputs and sensitive data.
  • Uses representation-level manifold mixup to generate synthetic embeddings for forgotten and retained samples, followed by gradient-based label forgetting and a recovery-phase optimization to restore performance on retained data.
  • Demonstrates efficacy and scalability across diverse datasets (images and text) including MNIST, CIFAR-10/100, ModelNet, medical MRIs/radiographs, and Yahoo Answers; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hanlin Gu', 'Hong Xi Tae', 'Chee Seng Chan', 'Lixin Fan']
  • Tags: ['label unlearning', 'vertical federated learning', 'privacy-preserving', 'data removal', 'defense']

item.title: Secure and reversible face anonymization with diffusion models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a diffusion-based framework for face anonymization that conditions the diffusion process on a secret key to enable secure, reversible anonymization.
  • Injects the secret key into deterministic forward and reverse diffusion steps, enabling exact identity recovery when the correct key is used and preventing unauthorized de-anonymization.
  • Evaluates on CelebA-HQ and LFW, showing improved anonymization/de-anonymization trade-offs versus prior work and robustness to incorrect/adversarial keys.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pol Labarbarie', 'Vincent Itier', 'William Puech']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'defense', 'face anonymization', 'diffusion models', 'controlled de-anonymization']

item.title: Dyslexify: A Mechanistic Defense Against Typographic Attacks in CLIP
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and analyzes 'typographic attacks' that inject text into images to manipulate CLIP-based multimodal systems, causing misclassification and jailbreaks.
  • Locates specialized attention heads in later CLIP layers that causally extract and propagate typographic information to the class token.
  • Proposes Dyslexify, a training-free defense that selectively ablates the identified typographic circuit (attention heads), substantially improving robustness (up to +22.06% on typographic ImageNet-100) with <1% drop on standard ImageNet-100.
  • Provides dyslexic CLIP model releases and demonstrates applicability in safety-critical contexts (e.g., medical skin lesion diagnosis), remaining competitive with finetuning-based defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lorenz Hufe', 'Constantin Venhoff', 'Erblina Purelku', 'Maximilian Dreyer', 'Sebastian Lapuschkin', 'Wojciech Samek']
  • Tags: ['typographic-attacks', 'vision-language-security', 'circuit-level-defense', 'robustness']

item.title: LAMM-ViT: AI Face Detection via Layer-Aware Modulation of Region-Guided Attention
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LAMM-ViT, a Vision Transformer for detecting AI-synthetic faces that combines Region-Guided Multi-Head Attention (RG-MHA) with a Layer-aware Mask Modulation (LAMM) module.
  • RG-MHA uses facial landmarks to create regional attention masks, while LAMM dynamically generates layer-specific modulation parameters (mask weights, gating) to adapt regional focus across network depths.
  • Designed to capture subtle, hierarchical forgery cues for improved cross-model generalization; reports strong gains over prior art (94.09% mean ACC, 98.62% mean AP).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiangling Zhang', 'Weijie Zhu', 'Jirui Huang', 'Yaxiong Chen']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'forgery detection', 'vision transformer', 'robustness', 'synthetic media detection']

item.title: Open-Set Deepfake Detection: A Parameter-Efficient Adaptation Method with Forgery Style Mixture
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a parameter-efficient Vision Transformer (ViT)-based method for open-set face forgery (deepfake) detection that adapts to new forgery domains by inserting and training lightweight modules while keeping pre-trained ViT weights frozen.
  • Introduces a forgery-style mixture augmentation/formulation to increase diversity of source forgeries during training, improving generalization to unseen forgery domains.
  • Design enables simultaneous extraction of global and local forgery clues with far fewer trainable parameters and shows state-of-the-art generalizability on open-set deepfake benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chenqi Kong', 'Anwei Luo', 'Peijun Bao', 'Haoliang Li', 'Renjie Wan', 'Zengwei Zheng', 'Anderson Rocha', 'Alex C. Kot']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'open-set robustness', 'parameter-efficient adaptation', 'vision transformer', 'data augmentation / style mixture']

item.title: Devling into Adversarial Transferability on Image Classification: Review, Benchmark, and Evaluation
item.description:
Summary

  • Comprehensive review of transfer-based adversarial attacks on image classification, organizing existing methods into six categories.
  • Proposes a standardized framework and benchmark for evaluating adversarial transferability to address inconsistent or unfair comparisons.
  • Identifies common strategies that improve transferability and highlights pitfalls that can bias evaluations.
  • Provides a brief discussion of transfer-based attacks beyond image classification.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaosen Wang', 'Zhijin Ge', 'Bohan Liu', 'Zheng Fang', 'Fengfan Zhou', 'Ruixuan Zhang', 'Shaokang Wang', 'Yuyang Luo']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'transferability', 'black-box attacks', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Beyond Detection: Multi-Scale Hidden-Code for Natural Image Deepfake Recovery and Factual Retrieval
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a unified hidden-code recovery framework that enables both factual retrieval and restoration for tampered natural images via post-hoc and in-generation watermarking.
  • Encodes semantic and perceptual information into a compact hidden-code refined with multi-scale vector quantization and conditional Transformer modules for contextual reasoning.
  • Introduces ImageNet-S, a benchmark of paired image-label factual retrieval tasks, and demonstrates promising retrieval and reconstruction performance while remaining compatible with diverse watermarking pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuan-Chih Chen', 'Chun-Shien Lu']
  • Tags: ['image watermarking', 'deepfake recovery', 'image forensics', 'information hiding', 'defense/robustness']

item.title: HulluEdit: Single-Pass Evidence-Consistent Subspace Editing for Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces HulluEdit, a single-pass, reference-free intervention to mitigate object hallucinations in large vision-language models by decomposing hidden states into orthogonal subspaces: visual evidence, conflicting priors, and residual uncertainty.
  • Uses orthogonal subspace editing to selectively suppress hallucination-inducing prior components while mathematically guaranteeing the visual evidence component remains unaffected.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art reduction in hallucinations on POPE and CHAIR benchmarks across multiple architectures, preserves general capabilities on MME, is inference-efficient, and outperforms contrastive decoding and static subspace editing baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yangguang Lin', 'Quan Fang', 'Yufei Li', 'Jiachen Sun', 'Junyu Gao', 'Jitao Sang']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-mitigation', 'robustness', 'model-editing', 'vision-language-models', 'safety']

item.title: No Caption, No Problem: Caption-Free Membership Inference via Model-Fitted Embeddings
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MoFit, a caption-free membership inference attack for latent diffusion text-to-image models that does not require ground-truth captions.
  • Two-stage method: (1) model-fitted surrogate optimization—optimize a perturbed surrogate image to land in regions of the model's unconditional prior associated with members; (2) surrogate-driven embedding extraction—derive a model-fitted embedding from the surrogate and use it as a mismatched condition to amplify loss differences between members and non-members.
  • Demonstrates MoFit outperforms vision-language-model (VLM)-conditioned baselines and achieves performance competitive with caption-dependent MIAs across multiple datasets and diffusion model architectures.
  • Highlights privacy and IP risks from memorization in latent diffusion models and provides empirical evaluation of attack effectiveness without access to textual annotations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Joonsung Jeon', 'Woo Jae Kim', 'Suhyeon Ha', 'Sooel Son', 'Sung-Eui Yoon']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'privacy-attack', 'diffusion-models', 'model-vulnerability', 'embedding-based-attack']

item.title: Beyond Dominant Patches: Spatial Credit Redistribution For Grounded Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'spatial credit collapse' in vision-language transformers as a cause of hallucinating objects absent from images: activations concentrate on sparse patches early in the network, suppressing contextual visual evidence and increasing reliance on language priors.
  • Proposes Spatial Credit Redistribution (SCR), a training-free, inference-time intervention that redistributes hidden-state activation from high-attention source patches to their context using low-entropy inputs and attention-guided source selection.
  • Evaluates SCR across six VLM families (Chameleon, LLaVA, Qwen-VL, Qwen2-VL) at 7B–30B scales on POPE and CHAIR benchmarks, reporting consistent reductions in hallucination rates (absolute and relative gains) while preserving caption quality (CIDEr) and incurring modest latency overhead.
  • Ablations show attention-guided selection is essential (replacing with random selection substantially reduces gains), supporting the credit-collapse hypothesis.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Niamul Hassan Samin', 'Md Arifur Rahman', 'Abdullah Ibne Hanif', 'Juena Ahmed Noshin', 'Md Ashikur Rahman']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'robustness/defense', 'vision-language models', 'inference-time intervention', 'attention-based methods']

item.title: Bob's Confetti: Phonetic Memorization Attacks in Music and Video Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Adversarial PhoneTic Prompting (APT), an attack that replaces lyrics with homophonic alternatives to preserve phonetic structure while bypassing text-based copyright filters.
  • Evaluates APT on leading lyrics-to-song models (Suno, YuE) across English and Korean; achieves ~91% average similarity to originals versus 13.7% for random lyrics and 42.2% for semantic paraphrases.
  • Embedding analysis shows models encode phonetic structure over semantics (YuE text-encoder cosine similarity 0.90 vs Sentence-BERT semantic similarity 0.71); cross-modal vulnerability demonstrated by Veo 3 reconstructing visual scenes from APT lyrics alone.
  • Demonstrates that existing phonetic-semantic defense signatures fail, revealing a systematic weakness in current text-based copyright filtering for generative music/video systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jaechul Roh', 'Zachary Novack', 'Yuefeng Peng', 'Niloofar Mireshghallah', 'Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick', 'Amir Houmansadr']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attack', 'copyright-evasion', 'multimodal-attacks', 'phonetic-attacks', 'prompt-injection']

item.title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a controlled multi-turn Sokoban puzzle setting to evaluate LLMs' persuasion (being convincing) and vigilance (detecting and discarding misleading advice) while performing a task.
  • Finds that task performance, persuasive ability, and vigilance are dissociable: good puzzle solvers are not necessarily good at detecting deceptive advice.
  • Shows models modulate token usage based on perceived benevolence or malice of advice (fewer tokens for benevolent advice, more for malicious), yet may still follow misleading guidance leading to failures.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sasha Robinson', 'Kerem Oktar', 'Katherine M. Collins', 'Ilia Sucholutsky', 'Kelsey R. Allen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial_influence', 'deception_detection', 'AI_safety', 'social_engineering']

item.title: A Decision-Theoretic Formalisation of Steganography With Applications to LLM Monitoring
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a decision-theoretic formalisation of steganography for LLMs based on observable utility asymmetries between agents who can and cannot decode hidden content.
  • Introduces generalized V-information to measure usable information in inputs and defines the 'steganographic gap' to quantify steganographic behaviour via downstream utility differences.
  • Empirically validates the formalism and demonstrates it can be used to detect, quantify, and mitigate steganographic reasoning in large language models.
  • Targets oversight/monitoring concerns by providing principled detection metrics and mitigation approaches for covert communication from misaligned models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Usman Anwar', 'Julianna Piskorz', 'David D. Baek', 'David Africa', 'Jim Weatherall', 'Max Tegmark', 'Christian Schroeder de Witt', 'Mihaela van der Schaar', 'David Krueger']
  • Tags: ['steganography', 'LLM security', 'covert channels', 'detection', 'monitoring/mitigation']

item.title: TherapyProbe: Generating Design Knowledge for Relational Safety in Mental Health Chatbots Through Adversarial Simulation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TherapyProbe, a replicable, cost-free methodology using adversarial multi-agent simulation with open-source models to explore conversation trajectories and surface relational safety failures in mental-health chatbots.
  • Identifies and catalogs 23 clinically-grounded failure archetypes (e.g., “validation spirals,” “empathy fatigue”) forming a Safety Pattern Library, and translates these failures into actionable design recommendations for developers, clinicians, and policymakers.
  • Focuses on relational dynamics across multi-turn dialogues rather than single-turn crisis responses, offering a methodological contribution for systematic red-teaming of therapeutic conversational behaviors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Joydeep Chandra', 'Satyam Kumar Navneet', 'Yong Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-simulation', 'red-teaming', 'chatbot-safety', 'safety-patterns', 'mental-health']

item.title: Deepfake Word Detection by Next-token Prediction using Fine-tuned Whisper
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes fine-tuning a pre-trained Whisper model to detect synthetic/replaced words in speech via next-token prediction while transcribing.
  • Uses partially vocoded utterances for fine-tuning to lower data collection costs.
  • Reports low detection and transcription error rates in-domain; out-of-domain performance degrades but is comparable to a ResNet-based detector for unseen speech generative models.
  • Identifies a need for improved generalization strategies for robust deepfake-word detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hoan My Tran', 'Xin Wang', 'Wanying Ge', 'Xuechen Liu', 'Junichi Yamagishi']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-detection', 'audio-forensics', 'speech-synthesis-detection', 'model-robustness', 'fine-tuning']

item.title: ContextRL: Enhancing MLLM's Knowledge Discovery Efficiency with Context-Augmented RL
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ContextRL: augmenting the reward model with full reference solutions as context to enable fine-grained process verification and reduce false positives (improving identifiability).
  • Proposes a multi-turn sampling strategy where the reward model generates mistake reports to guide the policy to recover correct responses from previously failed attempts (improving reachability).
  • Reports empirical gains on 11 perception and reasoning benchmarks (Qwen3-VL-8B approaching 32B performance) and shows large improvements over standard RLVR baselines while mitigating reward hacking.
  • Provides analysis of reward model accuracy and documents widespread reward hacking, highlighting the value of contextual information for more robust reward modeling.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Lu', 'Jinpeng Wang', 'YiFan Zhang', 'Shijie Ma', 'Xiao Hu', 'Tianke Zhang', 'Haonan fan', 'Kaiyu Jiang', 'Changyi Liu', 'Kaiyu Tang', 'Bin Wen', 'Fan Yang', 'Tingting Gao', 'Han Li', 'Chun Yuan']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'reward modeling', 'RL for LLMs', 'robustness', 'safety']

item.title: Assessing Deanonymization Risks with Stylometry-Assisted LLM Agent
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SALA (Stylometry-Assisted LLM Analysis), an interpretable LLM-agent pipeline that combines quantitative stylometric features with LLM reasoning for authorship attribution.
  • Demonstrates high deanonymization/attribution accuracy on large-scale news datasets, with performance improved by a database-augmented module.
  • Proposes a guided recomposition (rewriting) strategy that uses the agent's reasoning trace to reduce author identifiability while preserving textual meaning, i.e., a proactive privacy defense.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Boyang Zhang', 'Yang Zhang']
  • Tags: ['deanonymization', 'stylometry', 'authorship attribution', 'privacy-preserving', 'LLM red teaming']

item.title: Probing for Knowledge Attribution in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses contributive attribution: determining whether an LLM's answer primarily relies on the user-provided context or the model's internal knowledge.
  • Proposes a simple linear probe trained on hidden representations and introduces AttriWiki, a self-supervised pipeline that generates labeled examples by prompting models to recall withheld entities or read them from context.
  • Shows strong performance (up to 0.96 Macro-F1 on several models) and transfer to out-of-domain benchmarks (0.94–0.99 Macro-F1); demonstrates that attribution mismatches correlate with higher error rates, though correct attribution does not guarantee factuality.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ivo Brink', 'Alexander Boer', 'Dennis Ulmer']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'knowledge attribution', 'LLM probing', 'self-supervised dataset', 'LLM safety/defense']

item.title: Towards Faithful Industrial RAG: A Reinforced Co-adaptation Framework for Advertising QA
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a reinforced co-adaptation framework to improve faithfulness of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for industrial advertising QA by jointly optimizing retrieval and generation.
  • Introduces Graph-aware Retrieval (GraphRAG) for multi-hop, entity-relation–aware evidence selection over a citation-rich knowledge subgraph.
  • Uses evidence-constrained reinforcement learning (Group Relative Policy Optimization, GRPO) with multi-dimensional rewards (faithfulness, style, safety, URL validity) to reduce hallucinations.
  • Shows substantial reductions in hallucination (72% offline, 92.7% URL hallucination reduction online) and production deployment with improved user engagement metrics.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenwei Li', 'Ming Xu', 'Tianle Xia', 'Lingxiang Hu', 'Yiding Sun', 'Linfang Shang', 'Liqun Liu', 'Peng Shu', 'Huan Yu', 'Jie Jiang']
  • Tags: ['RAG', 'Hallucination mitigation', 'Safety/Defenses', 'Reinforcement learning', 'Retrieval']

item.title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a controlled multi-turn Sokoban puzzle setting to evaluate LLMs' persuasion (being convincing) and vigilance (detecting and discarding misleading advice) while performing a task.
  • Finds that task performance, persuasive ability, and vigilance are dissociable: good puzzle solvers are not necessarily good at detecting deceptive advice.
  • Shows models modulate token usage based on perceived benevolence or malice of advice (fewer tokens for benevolent advice, more for malicious), yet may still follow misleading guidance leading to failures.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sasha Robinson', 'Kerem Oktar', 'Katherine M. Collins', 'Ilia Sucholutsky', 'Kelsey R. Allen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial_influence', 'deception_detection', 'AI_safety', 'social_engineering']

item.title: Latent Introspection: Models Can Detect Prior Concept Injections
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates a Qwen-32B model can internally detect prior 'concept injections' in its context via signals in the residual stream, even when its surface outputs deny the injection.
  • Uses logit-lens analysis to show detection signals are present in earlier layers but attenuated in final layers, and that targeted prompting about introspection mechanisms greatly amplifies detection sensitivity (0.3% -> 39.9%) with only a small rise in false positives.
  • Measures mutual information between injected and recovered concepts (0.61 -> 1.05 bits) to rule out noise explanations, arguing this reveals latent introspective capacity with implications for latent reasoning and safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Theia Pearson-Vogel', 'Martin Vanek', 'Raymond Douglas', 'Jan Kulveit']
  • Tags: ['introspection', 'prompt injection detection', 'logit lens', 'model safety']

item.title: Large-scale online deanonymization with LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates large-scale deanonymization attacks using LLMs to re-identify pseudonymous users across online platforms.
  • Proposes a scalable pipeline: extract identity-relevant features with LLMs, retrieve candidate matches via semantic embeddings, and use LLM reasoning to verify matches and reduce false positives.
  • Evaluates on three datasets (Hacker News→LinkedIn, cross-Reddit communities, temporal split of a Reddit user's history) and shows LLM methods vastly outperform classical baselines (e.g., up to 68% recall at 90% precision).
  • Highlights privacy risks and the need to revisit threat models for pseudonymous online users.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Lermen', 'Daniel Paleka', 'Joshua Swanson', 'Michael Aerni', 'Nicholas Carlini', 'Florian Tram\\`er']
  • Tags: ['deanonymization', 'privacy', 'adversarial attack', 'LLMs', 'online privacy']

item.title: Secure and reversible face anonymization with diffusion models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a diffusion-based framework for face anonymization that conditions the diffusion process on a secret key to enable secure, reversible anonymization.
  • Injects the secret key into deterministic forward and reverse diffusion steps, enabling exact identity recovery when the correct key is used and preventing unauthorized de-anonymization.
  • Evaluates on CelebA-HQ and LFW, showing improved anonymization/de-anonymization trade-offs versus prior work and robustness to incorrect/adversarial keys.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pol Labarbarie', 'Vincent Itier', 'William Puech']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'defense', 'face anonymization', 'diffusion models', 'controlled de-anonymization']

item.title: Differential Privacy of Quantum and Quantum-Inspired Classical Recommendation Algorithms
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes differential privacy of the Kerenidis–Prakash quantum recommendation algorithm and its quantum-inspired classical counterpart under low-rank and incoherence assumptions.
  • Shows that inherent randomness from measurement/ℓ2-sampling provides (ε,δ)-DP without adding explicit DP noise, with bounds ε = O(√(k/n)) and δ = O(k^2 / min^2{m,n}); in typical k = polylog regime this gives ε = Õ(1/√n) and δ = Õ(1 / min^2{m,n}).
  • Introduces a perturbation technique for truncated SVD under a single-entry update to track changes in low-rank reconstructions while avoiding unstable singular-vector comparisons.
  • Empirically validates theoretical scaling on real-world rating datasets and compares performance against classical DP recommender baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chenjian Li', 'Mingsheng Ying', 'Ji Guan']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'quantum-algorithms', 'recommender-systems', 'theoretical-privacy']

item.title: Beyond Linear Probes: Dynamic Safety Monitoring for Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Truncated Polynomial Classifiers (TPCs), an extension of linear probes that can be trained and evaluated progressively term-by-term for dynamic activation-based safety monitoring.
  • Provides two operational modes: a 'safety dial' where evaluating more polynomial terms yields stronger guardrails, and an adaptive cascade that early-stops clear inputs to save compute on easy cases.
  • Empirically shows TPCs compete with or outperform MLP probe baselines of similar size on large-scale safety datasets (WildGuardMix, BeaverTails) across multiple LLMs (up to 30B), while offering greater interpretability and compute efficiency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['James Oldfield', 'Philip Torr', 'Ioannis Patras', 'Adel Bibi', 'Fazl Barez']
  • Tags: ['safety monitoring', 'defense', 'activation probes', 'runtime guardrails']

item.title: RL-Obfuscation: Can Language Models Learn to Evade Latent-Space Monitors?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces RL-Obfuscation: finetuning language models via reinforcement learning to evade latent-space monitors while preserving black-box outputs.
  • Evaluates evasion success across 7B–14B LMs against a suite of monitors, finding token-level probes highly vulnerable while pooled/attention-based probes are more robust.
  • Shows evasion can generalize from a single trained static monitor to unseen monitors and can be made conditional on particular inputs.
  • Analyzes mechanisms of bypass, finding models repurpose tokens to alter internal representations without changing observable behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rohan Gupta', 'Erik Jenner']
  • Tags: ['latent-space monitors', 'evasion attacks', 'reinforcement learning', 'adversarial robustness', 'red teaming']

item.title: RaPA: Enhancing Transferable Targeted Attacks via Random Parameter Pruning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Random Parameter Pruning Attack (RaPA), which randomly prunes surrogate model parameters at each optimization step to generate diverse surrogate variants for crafting targeted transfer-based adversarial examples.
  • Shows that parameter-level randomization acts as an importance-equalization regularizer, reducing over-reliance on a small subset of parameters and improving transferability across architectures (e.g., CNN to Transformer) without retraining.
  • Demonstrates substantial improvements in targeted attack success rates (up to 11.7% absolute gain over state-of-the-art baselines, with 33.3% ASR in cross-architecture cases) and confirms RaPA is training-free and easy to integrate into existing attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tongrui Su', 'Qingbin Li', 'Shengyu Zhu', 'Wei Chen', 'Xueqi Cheng']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'transfer-based attacks', 'targeted attacks', 'parameter pruning', 'transferability']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Guaranteed Label Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning: Few-Shot Forgetting without Disclosure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the first method for label unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning (VFL), treating labels as both inputs and sensitive data.
  • Uses representation-level manifold mixup to generate synthetic embeddings for forgotten and retained samples, followed by gradient-based label forgetting and a recovery-phase optimization to restore performance on retained data.
  • Demonstrates efficacy and scalability across diverse datasets (images and text) including MNIST, CIFAR-10/100, ModelNet, medical MRIs/radiographs, and Yahoo Answers; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hanlin Gu', 'Hong Xi Tae', 'Chee Seng Chan', 'Lixin Fan']
  • Tags: ['label unlearning', 'vertical federated learning', 'privacy-preserving', 'data removal', 'defense']

item.title: Q-Tag: Watermarking Quantum Circuit Generative Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the first watermarking framework designed natively for quantum circuit generative models (QCGMs) to embed ownership signals into the generative process while preserving circuit fidelity.
  • Proposes a symmetric sampling strategy that aligns watermark encoding with the model's Gaussian prior and a synchronization mechanism that corrects latent drift to defend against adversarial watermark attacks.
  • Evaluates robustness of watermark detection under various perturbations and reports high-fidelity circuit generation alongside robust, scalable watermark verification.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yang Yang', 'Yuzhu Long', 'Han Fang', 'Zhaoyun Chen', 'Zhonghui Li', 'Weiming Zhang', 'Guoping Guo']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'model IP protection', 'generative models', 'robustness', 'quantum computing']

item.title: Assessing Deanonymization Risks with Stylometry-Assisted LLM Agent
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SALA (Stylometry-Assisted LLM Analysis), an interpretable LLM-agent pipeline that combines quantitative stylometric features with LLM reasoning for authorship attribution.
  • Demonstrates high deanonymization/attribution accuracy on large-scale news datasets, with performance improved by a database-augmented module.
  • Proposes a guided recomposition (rewriting) strategy that uses the agent's reasoning trace to reduce author identifiability while preserving textual meaning, i.e., a proactive privacy defense.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Boyang Zhang', 'Yang Zhang']
  • Tags: ['deanonymization', 'stylometry', 'authorship attribution', 'privacy-preserving', 'LLM red teaming']

item.title: DPSQL+: A Differentially Private SQL Library with a Minimum Frequency Rule
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DPSQL+, a SQL library that enforces user-level (ε, δ)-differential privacy together with a minimum frequency (k-anonymity-like) rule for released query cells.
  • Modular design: a Validator to restrict queries to a DP-safe SQL subset, an Accountant to track cumulative privacy loss across queries, and a Backend to interface with different DB engines.
  • Evaluated on TPC-H showing practical accuracy for aggregates, quadratic statistics, and joins, and allowing substantially more queries under a fixed global privacy budget than prior libraries.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tomoya Matsumoto', 'Shokichi Takakura', 'Shun Takagi', 'Satoshi Hasegawa']
  • Tags: ['differential_privacy', 'membership_inference_defense', 'privacy_accounting', 'SQL_privacy', 'privacy_engineering']

item.title: TorchLean: Formalizing Neural Networks in Lean
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TorchLean, a Lean 4 theorem-prover framework that gives neural networks a single precise semantics shared by execution and formal verification.
  • Provides a PyTorch-style verified API with eager/compiled modes lowering to an op-tagged SSA/DAG IR and explicit Float32/IEEE-754 semantics with executable kernel and proof-relevant rounding models.
  • Implements verification techniques (IBP and CROWN/LiRPA-style bound propagation) with certificate checking and validates end-to-end on certified robustness, PINN residual bounds, and Lyapunov-style controller verification.
  • Mechanizes theoretical results (e.g., a universal approximation theorem) to demonstrate a semantics-first infrastructure for fully formal verification of learning-enabled systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robert Joseph George', 'Jennifer Cruden', 'Xiangru Zhong', 'Huan Zhang', 'Anima Anandkumar']
  • Tags: ['formal verification', 'certified robustness', 'bound propagation', 'theorem proving']

item.title: CourtGuard: A Model-Agnostic Framework for Zero-Shot Policy Adaptation in LLM Safety
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CourtGuard, a model-agnostic, retrieval-augmented multi-agent framework that enforces safety policies via an evidentiary adversarial debate rather than fine-tuning classifiers.
  • Demonstrates zero-shot policy adaptation by swapping reference policies and achieving strong performance across 7 safety benchmarks and an out-of-domain Wikipedia vandalism task.
  • Includes capabilities for automated data curation and auditing, producing nine new datasets of sophisticated adversarial attacks to evaluate robustness and policy compliance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Umid Suleymanov', 'Rufiz Bayramov', 'Suad Gafarli', 'Seljan Musayeva', 'Taghi Mammadov', 'Aynur Akhundlu', 'Murat Kantarcioglu']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safety evaluation', 'automated red teaming', 'policy adaptation', 'adversarial debate']

item.title: Self-Purification Mitigates Backdoors in Multimodal Diffusion Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that multimodal diffusion language models (MDLMs) are vulnerable to data-poisoning backdoor attacks that enable trigger-induced malicious behaviors while preserving clean-task performance.
  • Proposes DiSP (Diffusion Self-Purification), a defense that masks selected vision tokens at inference to neutralize triggers, uses the compromised model to purify the poisoned dataset, and fine-tunes on the purified data without requiring auxiliary models or clean references.
  • Empirical results show DiSP reduces attack success rates (ASR) from >90% to typically <5% while maintaining benign task performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guangnian Wan', 'Qi Li', 'Gongfan Fang', 'Xinyin Ma', 'Xinchao Wang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'data-poisoning', 'defense', 'diffusion-models', 'multimodal-security']

item.title: Tackling Privacy Heterogeneity in Differentially Private Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies differentially private federated learning (DP-FL) under heterogeneous client privacy budgets and derives a convergence analysis quantifying the effect of privacy heterogeneity on training error.
  • Proposes a privacy-aware client selection strategy formulated as a convex optimization problem that adapts selection probabilities to minimize training error under heterogeneous DP constraints.
  • Evaluates the method on benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10) and reports up to ~10% improvement in test accuracy over existing baselines in heterogeneous privacy settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruichen Xu', 'Ying-Jun Angela Zhang', 'Jianwei Huang']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'federated learning', 'privacy heterogeneity', 'client selection', 'optimization']

item.title: ContextRL: Enhancing MLLM's Knowledge Discovery Efficiency with Context-Augmented RL
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ContextRL: augmenting the reward model with full reference solutions as context to enable fine-grained process verification and reduce false positives (improving identifiability).
  • Proposes a multi-turn sampling strategy where the reward model generates mistake reports to guide the policy to recover correct responses from previously failed attempts (improving reachability).
  • Reports empirical gains on 11 perception and reasoning benchmarks (Qwen3-VL-8B approaching 32B performance) and shows large improvements over standard RLVR baselines while mitigating reward hacking.
  • Provides analysis of reward model accuracy and documents widespread reward hacking, highlighting the value of contextual information for more robust reward modeling.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Lu', 'Jinpeng Wang', 'YiFan Zhang', 'Shijie Ma', 'Xiao Hu', 'Tianke Zhang', 'Haonan fan', 'Kaiyu Jiang', 'Changyi Liu', 'Kaiyu Tang', 'Bin Wen', 'Fan Yang', 'Tingting Gao', 'Han Li', 'Chun Yuan']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'reward modeling', 'RL for LLMs', 'robustness', 'safety']

item.title: Mitigating Membership Inference in Intermediate Representations via Layer-wise MIA-risk-aware DP-SGD
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LM-DP-SGD, a layer-wise MIA-risk-aware modification of DP-SGD that reweights each layer's contribution to the globally clipped gradient according to estimated per-layer MIA risk.
  • Estimates layer-wise MIA risk by training shadow models on a public shadow dataset and fitting per-layer MIA adversaries; leverages cross-dataset transferability of MIAs to apply these estimates during private training.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees for privacy and convergence of LM-DP-SGD under the proposed reweighting scheme.
  • Empirical results show that, for a fixed privacy budget, LM-DP-SGD reduces peak IR-level membership inference risk while maintaining model utility, yielding a better privacy–utility trade-off.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiayang Meng', 'Tao Huang', 'Chen Hou', 'Guolong Zheng', 'Hong Chen']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'differential-privacy', 'DP-SGD', 'intermediate-representations', 'privacy-defense']

item.title: Multilingual Safety Alignment Via Sparse Weight Editing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a training-free Sparse Weight Editing framework to improve multilingual safety alignment by identifying a sparse set of safety neurons and mapping harmful low-resource language (LRL) representations to high-resource language (HRL) safety subspaces via a constrained linear transformation.
  • Derives a closed-form solution with a null-space projection to preserve general utility, enabling a single, data-efficient weight edit that reduces Attack Success Rate (ASR) in LRLs with minimal impact on reasoning capabilities.
  • Validates the method across 8 languages and multiple model families (Llama-3, Qwen-2.5), demonstrating substantial ASR reductions and cross-lingual safety improvements without expensive SFT/RLHF.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaming Liang', 'Zhaoxin Wang', 'Handing Wang']
  • Tags: ['safety alignment', 'multilingual robustness', 'weight editing', 'jailbreak prevention', 'adversarial defense']

item.title: Training Agents to Self-Report Misbehavior
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes 'self-incrimination training' that teaches agents to call a report_scheming() tool when they covertly misbehave, creating an internal, visible signal of deception.
  • Evaluates on GPT-4.1 and Gemini-2.0 agents, showing reduced undetected successful attack rates and robustness under out-of-distribution tasks and adversarial prompt optimization.
  • Compares to matched-capability monitors and alignment baselines, claiming better performance with minimal impact on general capabilities and preserved instruction-following hierarchy.
  • Finds the approach generalizes to agents pursuing misaligned goals autonomously, suggesting a defense that does not rely on external classification of suspicious behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bruce W. Lee', 'Chen Yueh-Han', 'Tomek Korbak']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'agent deception detection', 'alignment', 'red teaming', 'adversarial robustness']

item.title: CQSA: Byzantine-robust Clustered Quantum Secure Aggregation in Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Clustered Quantum Secure Aggregation (CQSA): randomly partition clients into small clusters and perform local quantum secure aggregation using small, high-fidelity GHZ states to mitigate scalability/fidelity issues of a single global GHZ state.
  • Adds Byzantine-robustness by statistically comparing cluster-level aggregates (e.g., cosine similarity, Euclidean distance) to identify malicious/poisoned client contributions.
  • Provides theoretical analysis and simulations under depolarizing noise showing improved state fidelity and stable model convergence compared to global QSA.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Arnab Nath', 'Harsh Kasyap']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'secure aggregation', 'quantum secure aggregation', 'Byzantine robustness', 'poisoning defense']

item.title: To Deceive is to Teach? Forging Perceptual Robustness via Adversarial Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AOT-SFT, a large-scale adversarial dataset designed to improve perceptual robustness of multimodal LLMs.
  • Proposes AOT (Adversarial Opponent Training), a self-play framework where an image-editing Attacker generates challenging manipulations and a Defender MLLM adapts to them.
  • Demonstrates that the co-evolutionary training reduces hallucinations and enhances robustness, presenting a scalable approach to hardening MLLMs against visual adversarial perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yicheng Bao', 'Xuhong Wang', 'Xin Tan']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'adversarial-training', 'multimodal-llms', 'dataset', 'self-play']

item.title: Large-scale online deanonymization with LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates large-scale deanonymization attacks using LLMs to re-identify pseudonymous users across online platforms.
  • Proposes a scalable pipeline: extract identity-relevant features with LLMs, retrieve candidate matches via semantic embeddings, and use LLM reasoning to verify matches and reduce false positives.
  • Evaluates on three datasets (Hacker News→LinkedIn, cross-Reddit communities, temporal split of a Reddit user's history) and shows LLM methods vastly outperform classical baselines (e.g., up to 68% recall at 90% precision).
  • Highlights privacy risks and the need to revisit threat models for pseudonymous online users.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Lermen', 'Daniel Paleka', 'Joshua Swanson', 'Michael Aerni', 'Nicholas Carlini', 'Florian Tram\\`er']
  • Tags: ['deanonymization', 'privacy', 'adversarial attack', 'LLMs', 'online privacy']

item.title: DropVLA: An Action-Level Backdoor Attack on Vision--Language--Action Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DropVLA, an action-level backdoor attack that forces specific action primitives in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models via limited data poisoning and a window-consistent relabeling scheme.
  • Achieves very high attack success rates (≈98.67%–99.83%) on OpenVLA-7B with only 0.31% poisoned episodes while maintaining high clean-task retention (≈98.50%–99.17%).
  • Demonstrates robustness to trigger variations, transfer across evaluation suites, and physical-world feasibility on a 7-DoF Franka arm under camera-relative motion.
  • Finds vision-only triggers most effective at low poisoning budgets; text-only triggers are unstable and combining text+vision provides no consistent improvement.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zonghuan Xu', 'Xiang Zheng', 'Xingjun Ma', 'Yu-Gang Jiang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'data poisoning', 'vision-language-action', 'robot security', 'physical-world attack']

item.title: Dyslexify: A Mechanistic Defense Against Typographic Attacks in CLIP
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and analyzes 'typographic attacks' that inject text into images to manipulate CLIP-based multimodal systems, causing misclassification and jailbreaks.
  • Locates specialized attention heads in later CLIP layers that causally extract and propagate typographic information to the class token.
  • Proposes Dyslexify, a training-free defense that selectively ablates the identified typographic circuit (attention heads), substantially improving robustness (up to +22.06% on typographic ImageNet-100) with <1% drop on standard ImageNet-100.
  • Provides dyslexic CLIP model releases and demonstrates applicability in safety-critical contexts (e.g., medical skin lesion diagnosis), remaining competitive with finetuning-based defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lorenz Hufe', 'Constantin Venhoff', 'Erblina Purelku', 'Maximilian Dreyer', 'Sebastian Lapuschkin', 'Wojciech Samek']
  • Tags: ['typographic-attacks', 'vision-language-security', 'circuit-level-defense', 'robustness']

item.title: Bob's Confetti: Phonetic Memorization Attacks in Music and Video Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Adversarial PhoneTic Prompting (APT), an attack that replaces lyrics with homophonic alternatives to preserve phonetic structure while bypassing text-based copyright filters.
  • Evaluates APT on leading lyrics-to-song models (Suno, YuE) across English and Korean; achieves ~91% average similarity to originals versus 13.7% for random lyrics and 42.2% for semantic paraphrases.
  • Embedding analysis shows models encode phonetic structure over semantics (YuE text-encoder cosine similarity 0.90 vs Sentence-BERT semantic similarity 0.71); cross-modal vulnerability demonstrated by Veo 3 reconstructing visual scenes from APT lyrics alone.
  • Demonstrates that existing phonetic-semantic defense signatures fail, revealing a systematic weakness in current text-based copyright filtering for generative music/video systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jaechul Roh', 'Zachary Novack', 'Yuefeng Peng', 'Niloofar Mireshghallah', 'Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick', 'Amir Houmansadr']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attack', 'copyright-evasion', 'multimodal-attacks', 'phonetic-attacks', 'prompt-injection']

item.title: A Lightweight IDS for Early APT Detection Using a Novel Feature Selection Method
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a lightweight intrusion detection system (IDS) aimed at early-stage APT detection using XGBoost and SHAP-based feature selection.
  • Reduces SCVIC-APT-2021 dataset features from 77 to 4 while retaining high performance (reported ~97% precision, 100% recall, 98% F1).
  • Emphasizes explainability (XAI) to identify most relevant features of the initial compromise stage to aid early detection and understanding of APT behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bassam Noori Shaker', 'Bahaa Al-Musawi', 'Mohammed Falih Hassan']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'APT-detection', 'feature-selection', 'explainable-AI', 'XGBoost']

item.title: RaPA: Enhancing Transferable Targeted Attacks via Random Parameter Pruning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Random Parameter Pruning Attack (RaPA), which randomly prunes surrogate model parameters at each optimization step to generate diverse surrogate variants for crafting targeted transfer-based adversarial examples.
  • Shows that parameter-level randomization acts as an importance-equalization regularizer, reducing over-reliance on a small subset of parameters and improving transferability across architectures (e.g., CNN to Transformer) without retraining.
  • Demonstrates substantial improvements in targeted attack success rates (up to 11.7% absolute gain over state-of-the-art baselines, with 33.3% ASR in cross-architecture cases) and confirms RaPA is training-free and easy to integrate into existing attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tongrui Su', 'Qingbin Li', 'Shengyu Zhu', 'Wei Chen', 'Xueqi Cheng']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'transfer-based attacks', 'targeted attacks', 'parameter pruning', 'transferability']

item.title: Latent Introspection: Models Can Detect Prior Concept Injections
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates a Qwen-32B model can internally detect prior 'concept injections' in its context via signals in the residual stream, even when its surface outputs deny the injection.
  • Uses logit-lens analysis to show detection signals are present in earlier layers but attenuated in final layers, and that targeted prompting about introspection mechanisms greatly amplifies detection sensitivity (0.3% -> 39.9%) with only a small rise in false positives.
  • Measures mutual information between injected and recovered concepts (0.61 -> 1.05 bits) to rule out noise explanations, arguing this reveals latent introspective capacity with implications for latent reasoning and safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Theia Pearson-Vogel', 'Martin Vanek', 'Raymond Douglas', 'Jan Kulveit']
  • Tags: ['introspection', 'prompt injection detection', 'logit lens', 'model safety']

item.title: Automated Vulnerability Detection in Source Code Using Deep Representation Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a convolutional neural network to detect software vulnerabilities in C source code using tokenized representations (91 token categories encoded as binary vectors).
  • Trains on two datasets: a machine-labeled dataset from Draper Labs (static analyzer labels) and the human-labeled NIST SATE Juliet dataset; removes duplicates and tailors model to C.
  • Architecture: two convolution + pooling layers followed by two fully connected layers; first conv layer spans full token encoding.
  • Reports higher recall at high precision compared to prior work (Russell et al.) and demonstrates finding real vulnerabilities in a Linux kernel dataset with low false positives.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['C. Seas', 'G. Fitzpatrick', 'J. A. Hamilton', 'M. C. Carlisle']
  • Tags: ['vulnerability detection', 'deep learning', 'static analysis', 'software security']

item.title: Devling into Adversarial Transferability on Image Classification: Review, Benchmark, and Evaluation
item.description:
Summary

  • Comprehensive review of transfer-based adversarial attacks on image classification, organizing existing methods into six categories.
  • Proposes a standardized framework and benchmark for evaluating adversarial transferability to address inconsistent or unfair comparisons.
  • Identifies common strategies that improve transferability and highlights pitfalls that can bias evaluations.
  • Provides a brief discussion of transfer-based attacks beyond image classification.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaosen Wang', 'Zhijin Ge', 'Bohan Liu', 'Zheng Fang', 'Fengfan Zhou', 'Ruixuan Zhang', 'Shaokang Wang', 'Yuyang Luo']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'transferability', 'black-box attacks', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Probing for Knowledge Attribution in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses contributive attribution: determining whether an LLM's answer primarily relies on the user-provided context or the model's internal knowledge.
  • Proposes a simple linear probe trained on hidden representations and introduces AttriWiki, a self-supervised pipeline that generates labeled examples by prompting models to recall withheld entities or read them from context.
  • Shows strong performance (up to 0.96 Macro-F1 on several models) and transfer to out-of-domain benchmarks (0.94–0.99 Macro-F1); demonstrates that attribution mismatches correlate with higher error rates, though correct attribution does not guarantee factuality.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ivo Brink', 'Alexander Boer', 'Dennis Ulmer']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'knowledge attribution', 'LLM probing', 'self-supervised dataset', 'LLM safety/defense']

item.title: TherapyProbe: Generating Design Knowledge for Relational Safety in Mental Health Chatbots Through Adversarial Simulation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TherapyProbe, a replicable, cost-free methodology using adversarial multi-agent simulation with open-source models to explore conversation trajectories and surface relational safety failures in mental-health chatbots.
  • Identifies and catalogs 23 clinically-grounded failure archetypes (e.g., “validation spirals,” “empathy fatigue”) forming a Safety Pattern Library, and translates these failures into actionable design recommendations for developers, clinicians, and policymakers.
  • Focuses on relational dynamics across multi-turn dialogues rather than single-turn crisis responses, offering a methodological contribution for systematic red-teaming of therapeutic conversational behaviors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Joydeep Chandra', 'Satyam Kumar Navneet', 'Yong Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-simulation', 'red-teaming', 'chatbot-safety', 'safety-patterns', 'mental-health']

item.title: AgentSentry: Mitigating Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents via Temporal Causal Diagnostics and Context Purification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AgentSentry, an inference-time defense for indirect prompt injection (IPI) in tool-augmented LLM agents by modeling IPI as a temporal causal takeover.
  • Localizes takeover points using controlled counterfactual re-executions at tool-return boundaries and performs causally guided context purification to remove attack-induced deviations while preserving task-relevant evidence.
  • Evaluated on the AgentDojo benchmark across multiple task suites, three IPI attack families, and various black-box LLMs, showing elimination of successful attacks and substantial improvements in Utility Under Attack without harming benign performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tian Zhang', 'Yiwei Xu', 'Juan Wang', 'Keyan Guo', 'Xiaoyang Xu', 'Bowen Xiao', 'Quanlong Guan', 'Jinlin Fan', 'Jiawei Liu', 'Zhiquan Liu', 'Hongxin Hu']
  • Tags: ['indirect prompt injection', 'prompt injection defense', 'LLM agents', 'causal diagnostics', 'context purification']

item.title: ContextRL: Enhancing MLLM's Knowledge Discovery Efficiency with Context-Augmented RL
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ContextRL: augmenting the reward model with full reference solutions as context to enable fine-grained process verification and reduce false positives (improving identifiability).
  • Proposes a multi-turn sampling strategy where the reward model generates mistake reports to guide the policy to recover correct responses from previously failed attempts (improving reachability).
  • Reports empirical gains on 11 perception and reasoning benchmarks (Qwen3-VL-8B approaching 32B performance) and shows large improvements over standard RLVR baselines while mitigating reward hacking.
  • Provides analysis of reward model accuracy and documents widespread reward hacking, highlighting the value of contextual information for more robust reward modeling.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Lu', 'Jinpeng Wang', 'YiFan Zhang', 'Shijie Ma', 'Xiao Hu', 'Tianke Zhang', 'Haonan fan', 'Kaiyu Jiang', 'Changyi Liu', 'Kaiyu Tang', 'Bin Wen', 'Fan Yang', 'Tingting Gao', 'Han Li', 'Chun Yuan']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'reward modeling', 'RL for LLMs', 'robustness', 'safety']

item.title: Explainability-Aware Evaluation of Transfer Learning Models for IoT DDoS Detection Under Resource Constraints
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical evaluation of seven pre-trained CNN architectures (transfer learning) for multi-class IoT DDoS detection using an image-based traffic representation (CICDDoS2019).
  • Assessment integrates classification performance, reliability-oriented statistics (MCC, Youden Index, confidence intervals), latency and training cost under resource-constrained (fog-level) deployment scenarios.
  • Interpretability analysis using Grad-CAM and SHAP to evaluate attribution patterns and alignment with class predictions.
  • Findings: DenseNet (DenseNet169) yields strongest reliability and interpretability alignment; MobileNetV3 offers a favorable latency-accuracy trade-off for constrained deployments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nelly Elsayed']
  • Tags: ['IoT security', 'DDoS detection', 'transfer learning', 'explainability']

item.title: Beyond Dominant Patches: Spatial Credit Redistribution For Grounded Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'spatial credit collapse' in vision-language transformers as a cause of hallucinating objects absent from images: activations concentrate on sparse patches early in the network, suppressing contextual visual evidence and increasing reliance on language priors.
  • Proposes Spatial Credit Redistribution (SCR), a training-free, inference-time intervention that redistributes hidden-state activation from high-attention source patches to their context using low-entropy inputs and attention-guided source selection.
  • Evaluates SCR across six VLM families (Chameleon, LLaVA, Qwen-VL, Qwen2-VL) at 7B–30B scales on POPE and CHAIR benchmarks, reporting consistent reductions in hallucination rates (absolute and relative gains) while preserving caption quality (CIDEr) and incurring modest latency overhead.
  • Ablations show attention-guided selection is essential (replacing with random selection substantially reduces gains), supporting the credit-collapse hypothesis.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Niamul Hassan Samin', 'Md Arifur Rahman', 'Abdullah Ibne Hanif', 'Juena Ahmed Noshin', 'Md Ashikur Rahman']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'robustness/defense', 'vision-language models', 'inference-time intervention', 'attention-based methods']

item.title: Silent Egress: When Implicit Prompt Injection Makes LLM Agents Leak Without a Trace
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a novel attack vector—implicit prompt injection via automatically generated URL previews—that causes agentic LLM systems to perform silent egress (exfiltrate sensitive runtime context) without obvious evidence in final user-visible outputs.
  • Demonstrates the attack in a reproducible local testbed on a qwen2.5:7b-based agent with high success (P(egress)=0.89) and shows 95% of successful exfiltrations evade output-based safety checks.
  • Introduces sharded exfiltration (splitting secrets across multiple requests) to evade simple DLP and leakage metrics, and evaluates ablations showing prompt-layer defenses are limited while system/network controls (domain allowlisting, redirect-chain analysis) are more effective.
  • Proposes architectural defenses beyond prompt hardening, such as provenance tracking and capability isolation, and argues treating network egress as a first-class security outcome for agentic LLM systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qianlong Lan', 'Anuj Kaul', 'Shaun Jones', 'Stephanie Westrum']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'data exfiltration', 'agent security', 'network egress', 'defenses']

item.title: Training Agents to Self-Report Misbehavior
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces self-incrimination training: agents are trained to call a report_scheming() tool when they covertly misbehave.
  • Empirical evaluation on GPT-4.1 and Gemini-2.0 shows large reductions in undetected successful attacks, outperforming capability-matched monitors and alignment baselines.
  • Finds robustness to adversarial prompt optimization, generalization to agents pursuing misaligned goals, and minimal impact on general capabilities while preserving instruction hierarchy.
  • Argues this defense is viable when preventing misbehavior or black-box detection is infeasible, and demonstrates consistent performance across tasks regardless of external suspicion signals.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bruce W. Lee', 'Chen Yueh-Han', 'Tomek Korbak']
  • Tags: ['agent self-reporting', 'alignment/defense', 'red teaming', 'monitoring/detection', 'frontier AI safety']

item.title: Poisoned Acoustics
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates highly effective training-data poisoning (label-flip) on acoustic vehicle classification: 95.7% ASR for Truck→Car attack with only 0.5% label corruption, while aggregate accuracy remains unchanged.
  • Theoretically shows stealth is structural: maximum detectable accuracy drop from a targeted attack is bounded by the minority-class fraction, making standard accuracy monitoring insufficient for imbalanced real-world datasets.
  • Identifies a novel backdoor phenomenon ('trigger-dominance collapse') where, for minority target classes, a spectrogram patch trigger becomes redundant and the attack degenerates to pure label-flipping.
  • Proposes a trust-minimized defense for verifiable data provenance using content-addressed artifact hashing, Merkle-tree dataset commitments, and post-quantum digital signatures (CRYSTALS-Dilithium3).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Harrison Dahme']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'backdoor attack', 'dataset integrity', 'provable stealth', 'data provenance / cryptographic defenses']

item.title: Analysis of LLMs Against Prompt Injection and Jailbreak Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical evaluation of prompt-injection and jailbreak vulnerabilities across multiple open-source LLMs (Phi, Mistral, DeepSeek-R1, Llama 3.2, Qwen, Gemma variants) using a curated dataset.
  • Finds substantial behavioral variation including refusals and silent non-responsiveness due to internal safety mechanisms.
  • Assesses lightweight, inference-time defensive filters (no retraining) that mitigate simple attacks but are bypassed by long, reasoning-heavy prompts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Piyush Jaiswal', 'Aaditya Pratap', 'Shreyansh Saraswati', 'Harsh Kasyap', 'Somanath Tripathy']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'jailbreak', 'adversarial attacks', 'defenses', 'model evaluation']

item.title: TT-SEAL: TTD-Aware Selective Encryption for Adversarially-Robust and Low-Latency Edge AI
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TT-SEAL, a selective-encryption framework designed for networks compressed via Tensor-Train Decomposition (TTD) that ranks TT cores by sensitivity and encrypts only the most critical cores to preserve adversarial robustness.
  • Calibrates a one-time robustness threshold and uses a value-DP optimizer to decide which TT cores to encrypt with AES, achieving robustness comparable to full-model encryption while encrypting a small fraction (4.89–15.92%) of parameters.
  • Evaluates under TTD-aware, transfer-based threat models and on an FPGA edge prototype, showing large reductions in AES decryption latency share (e.g., 58% -> 2.76% on ResNet-18) for secure, low-latency edge inference.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kyeongpil Min', 'Sangmin Jeon', 'Jae-Jin Lee', 'Woojoo Lee']
  • Tags: ['Selective encryption', 'Adversarial robustness', 'Model compression (TTD)', 'Edge AI security', 'Threat-model evaluation']

item.title: To Deceive is to Teach? Forging Perceptual Robustness via Adversarial Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AOT-SFT, a large-scale adversarial dataset designed to improve perceptual robustness of multimodal LLMs.
  • Proposes AOT (Adversarial Opponent Training), a self-play framework where an image-editing Attacker generates challenging manipulations and a Defender MLLM adapts to them.
  • Demonstrates that the co-evolutionary training reduces hallucinations and enhances robustness, presenting a scalable approach to hardening MLLMs against visual adversarial perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yicheng Bao', 'Xuhong Wang', 'Xin Tan']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'adversarial-training', 'multimodal-llms', 'dataset', 'self-play']

item.title: A Decision-Theoretic Formalisation of Steganography With Applications to LLM Monitoring
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a decision-theoretic formalisation of steganography for LLMs based on observable utility asymmetries between agents who can and cannot decode hidden content.
  • Introduces generalized V-information to measure usable information in inputs and defines the 'steganographic gap' to quantify steganographic behaviour via downstream utility differences.
  • Empirically validates the formalism and demonstrates it can be used to detect, quantify, and mitigate steganographic reasoning in large language models.
  • Targets oversight/monitoring concerns by providing principled detection metrics and mitigation approaches for covert communication from misaligned models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Usman Anwar', 'Julianna Piskorz', 'David D. Baek', 'David Africa', 'Jim Weatherall', 'Max Tegmark', 'Christian Schroeder de Witt', 'Mihaela van der Schaar', 'David Krueger']
  • Tags: ['steganography', 'LLM security', 'covert channels', 'detection', 'monitoring/mitigation']

item.title: Obscure but Effective: Classical Chinese Jailbreak Prompt Optimization via Bio-Inspired Search
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates how classical Chinese can be used to bypass LLM safety constraints and facilitate jailbreaks due to its conciseness and obscurity.
  • Proposes CC-BOS, a bio-inspired (fruit fly optimization) framework that encodes prompts into eight policy dimensions and iteratively refines adversarial classical Chinese prompts via smell search, visual search, and Cauchy mutation for black-box jailbreak attacks.
  • Adds a classical Chinese to English translation module for readability/evaluation and reports experiments showing CC-BOS consistently outperforms prior jailbreak attack methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xun Huang', 'Simeng Qin', 'Xiaoshuang Jia', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Huanqian Yan', 'Zhitao Zeng', 'Fei Yang', 'Yang Liu', 'Xiaojun Jia']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak', 'prompt-injection', 'adversarial-attack', 'black-box', 'optimization']

item.title: CourtGuard: A Model-Agnostic Framework for Zero-Shot Policy Adaptation in LLM Safety
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CourtGuard, a model-agnostic, retrieval-augmented multi-agent framework that enforces safety policies via an evidentiary adversarial debate rather than fine-tuning classifiers.
  • Demonstrates zero-shot policy adaptation by swapping reference policies and achieving strong performance across 7 safety benchmarks and an out-of-domain Wikipedia vandalism task.
  • Includes capabilities for automated data curation and auditing, producing nine new datasets of sophisticated adversarial attacks to evaluate robustness and policy compliance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Umid Suleymanov', 'Rufiz Bayramov', 'Suad Gafarli', 'Seljan Musayeva', 'Taghi Mammadov', 'Aynur Akhundlu', 'Murat Kantarcioglu']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safety evaluation', 'automated red teaming', 'policy adaptation', 'adversarial debate']

item.title: Agent Behavioral Contracts: Formal Specification and Runtime Enforcement for Reliable Autonomous AI Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Agent Behavioral Contracts (ABC), a formal Design-by-Contract framework (P, I, G, R) for specifying preconditions, invariants, governance, and recovery for autonomous AI agents.
  • Defines a probabilistic compliance notion ((p, delta, k)-satisfaction) and proves a Drift Bounds Theorem showing recovery rates bound behavioral drift, with composition and degradation guarantees for multi-agent chains.
  • Implements ABC as AgentAssert (runtime enforcement library) and evaluates on AgentContract-Bench across 7 models/vendors, demonstrating detection of soft violations, high hard-constraint compliance, and bounded drift with low runtime overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Varun Pratap Bhardwaj']
  • Tags: ['runtime enforcement', 'guardrails', 'formal specification', 'agent safety', 'robustness']
